title: XJ-2 airspeeder

The '''XJ-2 airspeeder''' was a customized [[Narglatch AirTech/Legends|Narglatch AirTech]] vehicle owned by [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] during his tenure as [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of the [[Alderaan sector/Legends|Alderaan sector]] and as [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] itself. After seeing fellow Senator [[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade's]] [[XJ-6 airspeeder/Legends|XJ-6 airspeeder]], Organa purchased his own custom [[Narglatch AirTech prefabricated kit]], which sported a red and silver [[Color/Legends|color]] scheme. The [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] became a hobby of Organa's, and he would tinker with it when he was not pressed to attend to [[Galactic Senate/Legends|senatorial]] duties. With the advent of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Organa took the XJ-2 with him to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where he found himself spending most of his [[Time/Legends|time]].

Organa registered the speeder under the alias of "Org Banelli," allowing him to operate without the attention of the authorities. Along with several other senators, Organa was considering ousting [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the conclusion of the war, and he needed to be able to move covertly. When the war did conclude, Organa used the speeder to rescue [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] from Palpatine's clutches. Returning to Alderaan, Organa found himself dedicated to forming the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and as a result, the speeder gathered dust. He gave it to his adopted daughter, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], and taught her [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] skills when she came of age. The speeder was eventually destroyed when the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|obliterated]] Alderaan, but a line based on the model was later put into production by Narglatch AirTech during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Characteristics==
A customized [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]], the XJ-2 was a small, two-seat vehicle that was 5.9 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length. The seats themselves were made of blue-hued leather. It sported a red and silver [[Color/Legends|color]] scheme, selected by [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], and in the hands of an able driver, the speeder could reach high and potentially hazardous speeds. As a precaution, the steering yoke was modified to allow for sharp turns. Though entirely new and constructed from a [[Narglatch AirTech prefabricated kit]], the vehicle was based on an older airspeeder model.<ref name="db" /><ref name="rotsvd">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> The original vehicle owned by Organa was unique at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of its conception and creation.<ref name="db" /> The later reproductions of the speeder were good at handling and boasted impressive speeds, and they excelled at both acceleration and climbing.<ref name="cort">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?id=66857&resource=features|text=''Corellia Times'' Consumer Reports: Vehicles}}</ref> A recolored variant of the speeder, used on [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] such as [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], sported a blue and yellow livery.<ref name="Explore: Front Runners">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/front-runners-episode-guide|text="Front Runners" Episode Guide | The Clone Wars}}</ref>

==History==
===Purchase and modifications===
[[Narglatch AirTech/Legends|Narglatch AirTech]], a small independent company, developed custom kits for airspeeder development. Their products were aimed at the upper class and more prestigious inhabitants of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], such as senators and [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] aristocracy. Senator [[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade]] of the [[Vorzyd sector/Legends|Vorzyd sector]] often flaunted his constructed [[XJ-6 airspeeder/Legends|XJ-6 airspeeder]] before his peers. Greyshade's displays inspired Senator Bail Organa of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to purchase and construct his own Narglatch AirTech kit.<ref name="db" />

Modification of the speeder became Organa's hobby, and when he was not being called upon to address matters of State, he could be found in his garage on Alderaan, working on the XJ-2. Deciding that it would be wise to keep a means of covert movement, Organa registered his speeder under the false name of "Org Banelli."<ref name="db" />

===Usage===
With the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] demanding Organa's full attention as a senator, he found little time to work as an aspiring [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]], and so the XJ-2 received less and less attention. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out, Organa spent most of his time on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and he brought his speeder along with him.<ref name="db" /><ref name="rotsn">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="ECR">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Organa learned from a [[Friends of the Republic|contact]] that the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were planning to destroy the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], so he flew his speeder to the [[Naboo Queen (restaurant)|apartment]] of Senator [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] to try and organize through her a meeting with the Order.<ref name="Wild Space">''[[The Clone Wars: Wild Space]]''</ref> Around [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]],<ref name="SW Annual 2011">''[[Star Wars Annual 2011]]''</ref> while working to defeat a [[Republic military enhancement bill/Legends|bill that would escalate the war effort]], Organa came under fire from the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]<ref name="Pursuit of Peace">{{TCW|Pursuit of Peace}}</ref> [[Robonino/Legends|Robonino]] and [[Chata Hyoki/Legends|Chata Hyoki]],<ref name="Pursuit of Peace Ep Guide">{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode311.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Pursuit of Peace}}</ref> who attacked him while he was preparing to leave from the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate's]] speeder [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. While [[Police droid/Legends|police droids]] and Alderaanian [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] worked to apprehend the hired guns, Organa attempted to leave the hangar in his XJ-2 speeder, only to crash it into the hangar entrance when the vehicle was hit by [[Laser/Legends|laser]] fire from Hyoki. Although Robonino and Hyoki were apprehended, Organa sustained injuries from the crash and was unable to deliver a speech to the Senate.<ref name="Pursuit of Peace" />

XJ-2 airspeeders also proved to be popular on worlds such as Onderon,<ref name="Explore: Front Runners" /> which was controlled by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. During the [[Battle of Onderon/Legends|Battle of Onderon]]<ref name="Front Runners">{{TCW|Front Runners}}</ref> of circa [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="Death Sentence 1">[[Darth Maul — Death Sentence 1|''Darth Maul — Death Sentence'' 1]]</ref> the [[Onderon rebels/Legends|Onderon rebels]]—operating against the Confederacy in the capital [[City/Legends|city]], [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]]—loaded an XJ-2 speeder with munitions, then left it in the path of a Separatist [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] patrol in one of Iziz's streets. Once the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] patrol arrived, the rebels detonated the bomb-laden speeder, manipulating the Separatists into dispatching reinforcements to the site in the form of an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]], which the rebels then used to destroy Iziz's [[Onderon power generator/Legends|power generator]].<ref name="Front Runners" />

[[File:Narglatchxj2.png|thumb|left|250px|Bail Organa in his XJ-2 airspeeder]]
During the war's progression, Organa grew weary of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] constant amendments to the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Constitution]], and he therefore met with other senators, such as [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and [[Giddean Danu/Legends|Giddean Danu]], for frequent airing of grievances. The "Banelli" ruse allowed him to escape the notice of Palpatine's offices and enabled him to travel to the designated meeting points with a minimum of fuss.<ref name="db" /><ref name="rotsn" /> As the war came to a sudden halt in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the XJ-2 saw considerable use during the chaos of [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]], part of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]. When fires broke out at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], Organa sped there in his speeder to investigate. He was stopped by [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]] [[Appo/Legends|CC-1119]], who advised him to leave. After watching CC-1119 and the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] gun down [[Zett Jukassa/Legends|Zett Jukassa]], a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], the horrified Organa fled the scene in his XJ-2.<ref name="rots">{{Film|III}}</ref>

In the following days, Organa witnessed Palpatine's [[Declaration of a New Order]]. Disgusted with what he saw, he aided and abetted [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda's]] [[Skirmish in the Senate|assassination attempt]] on now-[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine. The attempt failed, however, and Organa once more used the speeder to help Yoda escape the clutches of the Emperor's [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. The "Org Banelli" registration helped Organa avoid arrest and allowed him to return to Alderaan, with the speeder, in peace. For a short while, he resumed his modification of the vehicle, but the forging of the new [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] meant that the XJ-2 would eventually gather dust.<ref name="db" />

When Organa's adopted daughter, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], came of age, he gave her the XJ-2 as a [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]] present. Using the airspeeder, he taught her basic [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] skills, which were essential when handling such a high-speed and dangerous craft. When [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] decided to use Alderaan as a test for the highly destructive capabilities for the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], the XJ-2 was destroyed in the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|planet's explosion]].<ref name="db" /><ref name="anh">{{Film|IV}}</ref> The craft was later produced in larger numbers by Narglatch AirTech and saw use during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The speeder was considered classy, and those utilizing it were held in high regard.<ref name="gal">{{GalaxiesNGE}}</ref> The speeder was also covered in a consumer report from ''[[Corellia Times]]'' on the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]]. ''Corellia Times'' gave a good rating all round, with particular recommendation for the craft's acceleration and climbing ability.<ref name="cort" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:OnderonSpeederArt-FR.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Concept art for the recolored speeder in "Front Runners"]]
The XJ-2 airspeeder was created for [[George Lucas]]'s [[2005]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], where it was used as a mode of transport for Bail Organa's character. Lucas based the speeder on the [[Wikipedia:1948 Tucker Sedan|1948 Tucker Sedan]] and intended it to be a reference to [[Wikipedia:Tucker: The Man and His Dream|''Tucker: The Man and His Dream'']].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith">{{Film|III}} DVD commentary, Chapter 42</ref> The vehicle's backstory was later developed by "[[Master Devwi]]" through the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature "[[What's The Story?]]" Devwi's [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry explained that Organa's speeder was of the same model line as the XJ-6 airspeeder, which was featured in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. Through the XJ-6, Devwi made a connection to the greater [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] in his entry as well, including the character of Simon Greyshade<ref name="db" /> from [[1978]]'s [[Star Wars (1977) 18|''Star Wars'' (1977) 18]]; Greyshade had been associated with the XJ-6 by the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]].<ref name="hnn">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|56|news/13516_5.html|Senate Speeder Thief Arrested}}</ref>

The speeder was featured in the [[2008]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]'' set [[Champions of the Force (SWGTCG)|''Champions of the Force'']] as a "loot card," which players could redeem for an actual speeder in the [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|computer game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''.<ref name="Champions of the Force Card">{{SWGTCG|set=Champions of the Force (SWGTCG)|cardname=XJ-2 Airspeeder}}</ref> After being briefly featured in the [[2008]] novel ''[[The Clone Wars: Wild Space]]'',<ref name="Wild Space" /> the airspeeder appeared in "[[Pursuit of Peace]]," an episode in the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series' [[2010]] [[The Clone Wars: Season Three|third season]].<ref name="Pursuit of Peace Ep Guide" /> A recolored version of the speeder also appeared in the [[2012]] [[The Clone Wars: Season Five|fifth season]] episode "[[Front Runners]]."<ref name="Explore: Front Runners" />
